If no EndpointSlice can be found, a nil pointer\\n170: // will be returned.\\n171: func getSliceToFill(endpointSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, numEndpoints, maxEndpoints int) (slice *discovery.EndpointSlice) {\\n172: \\tclosestDiff := maxEndpoints\\n173: \\tvar closestSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice\\n174: \\tfor _, endpointSlice := range endpointSlices {\\n175: \\t\\tcurrentDiff \\t\\tdelete(endpointSlice.Annotations, v1.EndpointsLastChangeTriggerTime)\\n197: \\t}\\n198: }\\n199: \\n200: // ServiceControllerKey returns a controller key for a Service but derived from\\n201: // nil\\n211: }\\n212: \\n213: // setEndpointSliceLabels returns a map with the new endpoint slices labels and true if there was an update.\\n214: // Slices labels must be equivalent to the Service labels except for the reserved IsHeadlessService, LabelServiceName and LabelManagedBy labels\\n215: // Changes to IsHeadlessService, LabelServiceName and LabelManagedBy labels on the Service do not result in updates to EndpointSlice labels.\\n216: func setEndpointSliceLabels(logger klog.Logger, epSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, service *v1.Service, controllerName string) (map[string]string, bool) {\\n217: \\tupdated := false\\n218: \\tepLabels := make(map[string]string)\\n219: \\tsvcLabels := make(map[string]string)\\n220: \\n221: \\t// check if the endpoint slice and the service have the same labels\\n222: \\t// clone current slice labels except the reserved labels\\n223: \\tfor key, value := range epSlice.Labels {\\n224: \\t\\tif isReservedLabelKey(key) {\\n225: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n226: \\t\\t}\\n227: \\t\\t// copy endpoint slice labels\\n228: \\t\\tepLabels[key] = value\\n229: \\t}\\n230: \\n231: \\tfor key, value := range service.Labels {\\n232: \\t\\tif isReservedLabelKey(key) {\\n233: \\t\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Service using reserved endpoint slices label\\\", \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service), \\\"skipping\\\", key, \\\"label\\\", value)\\n234: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n235: \\t\\t}\\n236: \\t\\t// copy service labels\\n237: \\t\\tsvcLabels[key] = value\\n238: \\t}\\n239: \\n240: \\t// if the labels are not identical update the slice with the corresponding service labels\\n241: \\tif !apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(epLabels, svcLabels) {\\n242: \\t\\tupdated = true\\n243: \\t}\\n244: \\n245: \\t// add or remove headless label depending on the service Type\\n246: \\tif !isServiceIPSet(service) {\\n247: \\t\\tsvcLabels[v1.IsHeadlessService] = \\\"\\\"\\n248: \\t} else {\\n249: \\t\\tdelete(svcLabels, v1.IsHeadlessService)\\n250: \\t}\\n251: \\n252: \\t// override endpoint slices reserved labels\\n253: \\tsvcLabels[discovery.LabelServiceName] = service.Name\\n254: \\tsvcLabels[discovery.LabelManagedBy] = controllerName\\n255: \\n256: \\treturn svcLabels, updated\\n257: }\\n258: \\n259: // isReservedLabelKey return true if the label is one of the reserved label for slices\\n260: func isReservedLabelKey(label string) bool {\\n261: \\tif label == discovery.LabelServiceName ||\\n262: \\t\\tlabel == discovery.LabelManagedBy ||\\n263: \\t\\tlabel == v1.IsHeadlessService {\\n264: \\t\\treturn true\\n265: \\t}\\n266: \\treturn false\\n267: }\\n268: \\n269: // endpointSliceEndpointLen helps sort endpoint slices by the number of\\n270: // endpoints they contain.\\n271: type endpointSliceEndpointLen []*discovery.EndpointSlice\\n272: \\n273: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Len() int      { return len(sl) }\\n274: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Swap(i, j int) { sl[i], sl[j] = sl[j], sl[i] }\\n275: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Less(i, j int) bool {\\n276: \\treturn len(sl[i].Endpoints) > len(sl[j].Endpoints)\\n277: }\\n278: \\n279: // returns a map of address types used by a service\\n280: func getAddressTypesForService(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service) sets.Set[discovery.AddressType] {\\n281: \\tserviceSupportedAddresses := sets.New[discovery.AddressType]()\\n282: \\t// TODO: (khenidak) when address types are removed in favor of\\n283: \\t// v1.IPFamily this will need to be removed, and work directly with\\n284: \\t// v1.IPFamily types\\n285: \\n286: \\t// IMPORTANT: we assume that IP of (discovery.AddressType enum) is never in use\\n287: \\t// as it gets deprecated\\n288: \\tfor _, family := range service.Spec.IPFamilies {\\n289: \\t\\tif family == v1.IPv4Protocol {\\n290: \\t\\t\\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv4)\\n291: \\t\\t}\\n292: \\n293: \\t\\tif family == v1.IPv6Protocol {\\n294: \\t\\t\\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\\n295: \\t\\t}\\n296: \\t}\\n297: \\n298: \\tif serviceSupportedAddresses.Len() > 0 {\\n299: \\t\\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses // we have found families for this service\\n300: \\t}\\n301: \\n302: \\t// TODO (khenidak) remove when (1) dual stack becomes\\n303: \\t// enabled by default (2) v1.19 falls off supported versions\\n304: \\n305: \\t// Why do we need this:\\n306: \\t// a cluster being upgraded to the new apis\\n307: \\t// will have service.spec.IPFamilies: nil\\n308: \\t// if the controller manager connected to old api\\n309: \\t// server. This will have the nasty side effect of\\n310: \\t// removing all slices already created for this service.\\n311: \\t// this will disable all routing to service vip (ClusterIP)\\n312: \\t// this ensures that this does not happen. Same for headless services\\n313: \\t// we assume it is dual stack, until they get defaulted by *new* api-server\\n314: \\t// this ensures that traffic is not disrupted  until then. But *may*\\n315: \\t// include undesired families for headless services until then.\\n316: \\n317: \\tif len(service.Spec.ClusterIP) > 0 && service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone { // headfull\\n318: \\t\\taddrType := discovery.AddressTypeIPv4\\n319: \\t\\tif utilnet.IsIPv6String(service.Spec.ClusterIP) {\\n320: \\t\\t\\taddrType = discovery.AddressTypeIPv6\\n321: \\t\\t}\\n322: \\t\\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(addrType)\\n323: \\t\\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for service. This could happen if controller manager is connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. EndpointSlices for this Service will use addressType as the IP Family based on familyOf(ClusterIP).\\\", \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service), \\\"addressType\\\", addrType, \\\"clusterIP\\\", service.Spec.ClusterIP)\\n324: \\t\\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\\n325: \\t}\\n326: \\n327: \\t// headless\\n328: \\t// for now we assume two families. This should have minimal side effect\\n329: \\t// if the service is headless with no selector, then this will remain the case\\n330: \\t// if the service is headless with selector then chances are pods are still using single family\\n331: \\t// since kubelet will need to restart in order to start patching pod status with multiple ips\\n332: \\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv4)\\n333: \\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\\n334: \\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for headless service, likely because controller manager is likely connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. The service endpoint slice will use dual stack families until api-server default it correctly\\\", \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service))\\n335: \\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\\n336: }\\n337: \\n338: func unchangedSlices(existingSlices, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete []*discovery.EndpointSlice) []*discovery.EndpointSlice {\\n339: \\tchangedSliceNames := sets.New[string]()\\n340: \\tfor _, slice := range slicesToUpdate {\\n341: \\t\\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\\n342: \\t}\\n343: \\tfor _, slice := range slicesToDelete {\\n344: \\t\\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\\n345: \\t}\\n346: \\tunchangedSlices := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n347: \\tfor _, slice := range existingSlices {\\n348: \\t\\tif !changedSliceNames.Has(slice.Name) {\\n349: \\t\\t\\tunchangedSlices = append(unchangedSlices, slice)\\n350: \\t\\t}\\n351: \\t}\\n352: \\n353: \\treturn unchangedSlices\\n354: }\\n355: \\n356: // hintsEnabled returns true if the provided annotations include either\\n357: // v1.AnnotationTopologyMode or v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints key\\n358: // with a value set to \\\"Auto\\\" or \\\"auto\\\". When both are set,\\n359: // v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints has precedence.\\n360: func hintsEnabled(annotations map[string]string) bool {\\n361: \\tval, ok := annotations[v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints]\\n362: \\tif !ok {\\n363: \\t\\tval, ok = annotations[v1.AnnotationTopologyMode]\\n364: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n365: \\t\\t\\treturn false\\n366: \\t\\t}\\n367: \\t}\\n368: \\treturn val == \\\"Auto\\\" || val == \\\"auto\\\"\\n369: }\\n370: \\n371: // isServiceIPSet aims to check if the service's ClusterIP is set or not\\n372: // the objective is not to perform validation here\\n373: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper\\n374: func isServiceIPSet(service *v1.Service) bool {\\n375: \\treturn service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone && service.Spec.ClusterIP != \\\"\\\"\\n376: }\\n377: \\n378: // findPort locates the container port for the given pod and portName. \\t\\t\\t}\\n394: \\t\\t}\\n395: \\tcase intstr.Int:\\n396: \\t\\treturn portName.IntValue(), nil\\n397: \\t}\\n398: \\n399: \\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\\\"no suitable port for manifest: %s\\\", pod.UID)\\n400: }\\n401: result in updates to EndpointSlice labels.\\n216: func setEndpointSliceLabels(logger klog.Logger, epSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, service *v1.Service, controllerName string) (map[string]string, bool) {\\n217: \\tupdated := false\\n218: \\tepLabels := make(map[string]string)\\n219: \\tsvcLabels := make(map[string]string)\\n220: \\n221: \\t// check if the endpoint slice and the service have \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n226: \\t\\t}\\n227: \\t\\t// copy endpoint slice labels\\n228: \\t\\tepLabels[key] = value\\n229: \\t}\\n230: \\n231: \\tfor key, value := range service.Labels {\\n232: \\t\\tif corresponding service labels\\n241: \\tif !apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(epLabels, svcLabels) {\\n242: \\t\\tupdated = true\\n243: \\t}\\n244: \\n245: \\t// add or remove headless label depending on the service Type\\n246: \\tif !isServiceIPSet(service) {\\n247: \\t\\tsvcLabels[v1.IsHeadlessService] = \\\"\\\"\\n248: \\t} else {\\n249: \\t\\tdelete(svcLabels, v1.IsHeadlessService)\\n250: \\n256: \\treturn svcLabels, updated\\n257: }\\n258: \\n259: // isReservedLabelKey return true if the label is one of the reserved label for slices\\n260: func isReservedLabelKey(label string) bool {\\n261: \\tif label == discovery.LabelServiceName ||\\n262: \\t\\tlabel == discovery.LabelManagedBy ||\\n263: \\t\\tlabel == v1.IsHeadlessService {\\n264: \\t\\treturn true\\n265: \\t}\\n266: \\treturn false\\n267: }\\n268: \\n269: // endpointSliceEndpointLen helps sort endpoint slices by the number of\\n270: // endpoints they contain.\\n271: type endpointSliceEndpointLen []*discovery.EndpointSlice\\n272: \\n273: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Len() int      { return len(sl) }\\n274: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Swap(i, j int) { sl[i], sl[j] = sl[j], sl[i] service\\n280: func getAddressTypesForService(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service) sets.Set[discovery.AddressType] {\\n281: \\tserviceSupportedAddresses := sets.New[discovery.AddressType]()\\n282: \\t// TODO: (khenidak) when address types are removed in favor of\\n283: \\t// v1.IPFamily this will need to be removed, and work directly with\\n284: \\t// v1.IPFamily types\\n285: \\n286: \\t// IMPORTANT: we assume that IP of (discovery.AddressType enum) is never in use\\n287: \\t// as it gets deprecated\\n288: \\tfor _, family := range service.Spec.IPFamilies {\\n289: \\t\\tif family == \\t\\t\\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\\n295: \\t\\t}\\n296: \\t}\\n297: \\n298: \\tif serviceSupportedAddresses.Len() > 0 {\\n299: \\t\\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses // we have found families for this service\\n300: \\t}\\n301: \\n302: \\t// TODO (khenidak) remove when (1) dual stack becomes\\n303: \\t// enabled by default (2) v1.19 falls off supported versions\\n304: \\n305: \\t// Why do we need this:\\n306: \\t// a cluster being upgraded to the new apis\\n307: \\t// will have service.spec.IPFamilies: nil\\n308: \\t// if the controller manager connected to old api\\n309: \\t// server. This will have the nasty side effect of\\n310: \\t// removing all slices already created for this service.\\n311: \\t// this will disable all routing to service vip (ClusterIP)\\n312: \\t// this ensures that this does not happen. Same for headless services\\n313: \\t// we assume it is dual stack, until they get defaulted by *new* api-server\\n314: \\t// this ensures that traffic is not disrupted  until then. But *may*\\n315: \\t// include undesired families for headless services until then.\\n316: \\n317: \\tif len(service.Spec.ClusterIP) > 0 && service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone { // headfull\\n318: \\t\\taddrType := discovery.AddressTypeIPv4\\n319: \\t\\tif utilnet.IsIPv6String(service.Spec.ClusterIP) {\\n320: \\t\\t\\taddrType = discovery.AddressTypeIPv6\\n321: \\t\\t}\\n322: \\t\\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(addrType)\\n323: \\t\\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for service. This could happen if controller manager is connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. EndpointSlices for this Service will use addressType as the IP Family based on familyOf(ClusterIP).\\\", \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service), \\\"addressType\\\", addrType, \\\"clusterIP\\\", service.Spec.ClusterIP)\\n324: \\t\\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\\n325: \\t}\\n326: \\n327: \\t// headless\\n328: \\t// for now we assume two families. This should have minimal side effect\\n329: \\t// if the service is headless with no selector, then this will remain the case\\n330: \\t// if the service is headless with selector then chances are pods are still using single family\\n331: \\t// since kubelet will need to restart in order to start patching pod status with multiple ips\\n332: \\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv4)\\n333: \\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\\n334: \\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for headless service, likely because controller manager is likely connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. The service endpoint slice will use dual stack families until api-server default it correctly\\\", \\\"service\\\", klog.KObj(service))\\n335: \\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\\n336: }\\n337: \\n338: func unchangedSlices(existingSlices, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete []*discovery.EndpointSlice) []*discovery.EndpointSlice {\\n339: \\tchangedSliceNames := sets.New[string]()\\n340: \\tfor _, slice := range slicesToUpdate {\\n341: \\t\\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\\n342: \\t}\\n343: \\tfor _, slice := range slicesToDelete {\\n344: \\t\\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\\n345: \\t}\\n346: \\tunchangedSlices := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\\n347: \\tfor _, slice := range existingSlices {\\n348: \\t\\tif !changedSliceNames.Has(slice.Name) {\\n349: \\t\\t\\tunchangedSlices = append(unchangedSlices, slice)\\n350: \\t\\t}\\n351: \\t}\\n352: \\n353: \\treturn unchangedSlices\\n354: }\\n355: \\n356: // hintsEnabled returns true if the provided annotations include either\\n357: // v1.AnnotationTopologyMode or v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints key\\n358: // with a value set to \\\"Auto\\\" or \\\"auto\\\". When both are set,\\n359: // v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints has precedence.\\n360: func hintsEnabled(annotations map[string]string) bool {\\n361: \\tval, ok := annotations[v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints]\\n362: \\tif !ok {\\n363: \\t\\tval, ok = annotations[v1.AnnotationTopologyMode]\\n364: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n365: \\t\\t\\treturn false\\n366: \\t\\t}\\n367: \\t}\\n368: \\treturn val == \\\"Auto\\\" || val == \\\"auto\\\"\\n369: }\\n370: \\n371: // isServiceIPSet aims to check if the service's ClusterIP is set or not\\n372: // the objective is not to perform validation here\\n373: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper\\n374: func isServiceIPSet(service *v1.Service) bool {\\n375: \\treturn service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone && service.Spec.ClusterIP != \\\"\\\"\\n376: }\\n377: \\n378: // findPort locates the container port for the given pod and portName.  If the\\n379: // targetPort is a number, use that.  If the targetPort is a string, look that\\n380: // string up in all named ports in all containers in the target pod.  If no\\n381: // match is found, fail.\\n382: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\\n383: func findPort(pod *v1.Pod, svcPort *v1.ServicePort) (int, error) {\\n384: \\tportName := svcPort.TargetPort\\n385: \\tswitch portName.Type {\\n386: \\tcase intstr.String:\\n387: \\t\\tname := portName.StrVal\\n388: \\t\\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.Containers {\\n389: \\t\\t\\tfor _, port := range container.Ports {\\n390: \\t\\t\\t\\tif port.Name == name && port.Protocol == svcPort.Protocol {\\n391: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn int(port.ContainerPort), nil\\n392: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n393: \\t\\t\\t}\\n394: \\t\\t}\\n395: \\tcase intstr.Int:\\n396: \\t\\treturn portName.IntValue(), nil\\n397: \\t}\\n398: \\n399: \\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\\\"no suitable port for manifest: %s\\\", pod.UID)\\n400: }\\n401: and a Service for a particular addressType.\n37: func podToEndpoint(pod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node, service *v1.Service, addressType discovery.AddressType) discovery.Endpoint {\n38: \tserving := endpointutil.IsPodReady(pod)\n39: \tterminating := pod.DeletionTimestamp != nil\n40: \t// For compatibility reasons, \"ready\" should never be \"true\" if a pod is terminatng, unless\n41: \t// ports.\n80: \tif len(service.Spec.Ports) == 0 && service.Spec.ClusterIP == v1.ClusterIPNone {\n81: \t\treturn endpointPorts\n82: \t}\n83: \n84: \tfor i := range service.Spec.Ports {\n85: {\n112: \t\tisIPv6PodIP := utilnet.IsIPv6String(podIP.IP)\n113: \t\tif isIPv6PodIP && addressType == discovery.AddressTypeIPv6 {\n114: \t\t\taddresses = append(addresses, podIP.IP)\n115: \t\t}\n116: controllerName string) *discovery.EndpointSlice {\n128: \tgvk := schema.GroupVersionKind{Version: \"v1\", Kind: \"Service\"}\n129: \townerRef := metav1.NewControllerRef(service, gvk)\n130: \tepSlice EndpointSlice is owned by the provided\n158: // Service.\n159: func ownedBy(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, svc *v1.Service) bool {\n160: \tfor _, o := range endpointSlice.OwnerReferences {\n161: \t\tif o.UID == svc.UID && o.Kind == \"Service\" && o.APIVersion == \"v1\" {\n162: \t\t\treturn true\n163: \t\t}\n164: \t}\n165: \treturn false\n166: }\n167: \n168: // getSliceToFill will return the EndpointSlice that will be closest to full\n169: // when numEndpoints are added. If no EndpointSlice can be found, a nil pointer\n170: // will be returned.\n171: func getSliceToFill(endpointSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, numEndpoints, maxEndpoints int) (slice *discovery.EndpointSlice) {\n172: \tclosestDiff := maxEndpoints\n173: \tvar closestSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice\n174: \tfor _, endpointSlice := range endpointSlices {\n175: \t\tcurrentDiff := maxEndpoints - (numEndpoints + len(endpointSlice.Endpoints))\n176: \t\tif currentDiff >= 0 && currentDiff < closestDiff {\n177: \t\t\tclosestDiff = currentDiff\n178: \t\t\tclosestSlice = endpointSlice\n179: \t\t\tif closestDiff == 0 {\n180: \t\t\t\treturn closestSlice\n181: \t\t\t}\n182: \t\t}\n183: \t}\n184: \treturn closestSlice\n185: }\n186: \n187: // addTriggerTimeAnnotation adds a triggerTime annotation to an EndpointSlice\n188: func addTriggerTimeAnnotation(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) {\n189: \tif endpointSlice.Annotations == nil {\n190: triggerTime.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n195: \t} else { // No new trigger time, clear the annotation.\n196: \t\tdelete(endpointSlice.Annotations, v1.EndpointsLastChangeTriggerTime)\n197: \t}\n198: }\n199: \n200: // ServiceControllerKey returns a controller key for a Service but derived from\n201: // an EndpointSlice.\n202: func ServiceControllerKey(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice) \t}\n210: \treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", endpointSlice.Namespace, serviceName), nil\n211: }\n212: \n213: // setEndpointSliceLabels returns a map with the new endpoint slices labels and true if there was an update.\n214: // Slices labels must be equivalent to the Service labels except for the reserved IsHeadlessService, LabelServiceName and LabelManagedBy labels\n215: // Changes to IsHeadlessService, LabelServiceName and LabelManagedBy labels on the Service do not result in updates to EndpointSlice labels.\n216: func setEndpointSliceLabels(logger klog.Logger, epSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, service *v1.Service, controllerName string) (map[string]string, bool) {\n217: \tupdated := false\n218: \tepLabels := make(map[string]string)\n219: \tsvcLabels := make(map[string]string)\n220: \n221: \t// check if the endpoint slice and the service have the same labels\n222: \t// clone current slice labels except the reserved labels\n223: \tfor key, value := service Type\n246: \tif !isServiceIPSet(service) {\n247: \t\tsvcLabels[v1.IsHeadlessService] = \"\"\n248: \t} else {\n249: \t\tdelete(svcLabels, v1.IsHeadlessService)\n250: \t}\n251: \n252: \t// updated\n257: }\n258: \n259: // isReservedLabelKey return true if the label is one of the reserved label for slices\n260: func isReservedLabelKey(label string) bool {\n261: \tif label == discovery.LabelServiceName ||\n262: \t\tlabel == discovery.LabelManagedBy ||\n263: \t\tlabel == v1.IsHeadlessService {\n264: \t\treturn true\n265: \t}\n266: \treturn false\n267: }\n268: \n269: // endpointSliceEndpointLen helps sort endpoint slices by the number of\n270: // endpoints they contain.\n271: type endpointSliceEndpointLen []*discovery.EndpointSlice\n272: \n273: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Len() int      { return len(sl) }\n274: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Swap(i, j int) { sl[i], sl[j] = sl[j], sl[i] }\n275: func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Less(i, j int) bool {\n276: \treturn len(sl[i].Endpoints) > len(sl[j].Endpoints)\n277: }\n278: \n279: // returns a map of address types used by a service\n280: func getAddressTypesForService(logger klog.Logger, of\n283: \t// v1.IPFamily this will need to be removed, and work directly with\n284: \t// v1.IPFamily types\n285: \n286: \t// IMPORTANT: we assume that IP of (discovery.AddressType enum) is never in \t\t\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\n295: \t\t}\n296: \t}\n297: \n298: \tif serviceSupportedAddresses.Len() > 0 {\n299: \t\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses // we have found families for this service\n300: \t}\n301: \n302: \t// TODO (khenidak) remove when (1) dual stack becomes\n303: \t// enabled by default (2) v1.19 falls off supported versions\n304: \n305: \t// Why do we need this:\n306: \t// a cluster being upgraded to the new apis\n307: \t// will have service.spec.IPFamilies: nil\n308: \t// if the controller manager connected to old api\n309: \t// server. This will have the nasty side effect of\n310: \t// removing all slices already created for this service.\n311: \t// this will disable all routing to service vip (ClusterIP)\n312: \t// this ensures that this does not happen. Same for headless services\n313: \t// we assume it is dual stack, until they get defaulted by *new* api-server\n314: \t// this ensures that traffic is not disrupted  until then. But *may*\n315: \t// include undesired families for headless services until then.\n316: \n317: \tif len(service.Spec.ClusterIP) > 0 && service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone { // headfull\n318: \t\taddrType := discovery.AddressTypeIPv4\n319: \t\tif utilnet.IsIPv6String(service.Spec.ClusterIP) {\n320: \t\t\taddrType = discovery.AddressTypeIPv6\n321: \t\t}\n322: \t\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(addrType)\n323: \t\tlogger.V(2).Info(\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for service. This could happen if controller manager is connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. EndpointSlices for this Service will use addressType as the IP Family based on familyOf(ClusterIP).\", \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"addressType\", addrType, \"clusterIP\", service.Spec.ClusterIP)\n324: \t\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\n325: \t}\n326: \n327: \t// headless\n328: \t// for now we assume two families.
